error_codes_observed: FATAL: remaining connection slots are reserved
root_cause: Deployment CHG0071892 reduced HikariCP maxPoolSize from 150 to 40 on PaymentAPI. Under peak load the connection pool was exhausted causing HTTP 503 errors on PaymentAPI and PostgreSQL connection count reaching 94 percent of max_connections on TransactionDB. HikariCP reported pool timeout after 30 seconds wait.
resolution_steps: Restored HikariCP maxPoolSize to 150 via PaymentAPI actuator PATCH endpoint. Restarted connection manager pod. Monitored PostgreSQL active connections dropping below 70 percent threshold. Confirmed PaymentAPI HTTP 5xx rate returning to baseline.

root_cause: Slow query on TransactionDB caused connection hold time to increase significantly. HikariCP connections held open waiting for slow query results exhausted the pool. PaymentAPI HTTP 5xx rate spiked to 340 percent above baseline.
resolution_steps: Identified and killed slow running query on TransactionDB. Added query timeout of 30 seconds to HikariCP configuration. Restarted connection pool. Verified connection count normalised.

root_cause: Black Friday traffic volume 3x normal exhausted HikariCP connection pool. Pool size was not pre-scaled for peak traffic period. PaymentAPI and TransactionDB both showed connection exhaustion symptoms.
resolution_steps: Emergency increase of HikariCP maxPoolSize to 200. Monitored connection count recovery. Post-incident added auto-scaling trigger for pool size based on traffic metrics.

root_cause: Memory leak in session cache not releasing expired tokens. JVM heap reached 95 percent after 72 hours of uptime. Garbage collector unable to reclaim memory fast enough.
resolution_steps: Rolling restart of AuthService pods. Added TTL eviction policy to session cache. Increased JVM heap size as temporary measure.

root_cause: Concurrent batch settlement jobs acquiring row locks in inconsistent order causing deadlock cascade on TransactionDB. Multiple transactions waiting on each other.
resolution_steps: Killed long-running transactions. Serialised batch settlement jobs. Added index on settlement_date column to reduce lock contention.

root_cause: Email queue backlog caused in-memory buffer to grow unbounded. JVM heap exhausted after queue depth reached 50000 messages.
resolution_steps: Restarted NotificationService. Implemented bounded queue with dead-letter handling. Added queue depth monitoring alert.

root_cause: RDS replica fell behind primary by 45 seconds during high write load. Read queries routed to replica returned stale account balances.
resolution_steps: Temporarily routed all reads to primary. Investigated replication lag root cause. Resolved by reducing write batch size.

root_cause: Circular dependency in token validation chain caused StackOverflowError under specific JWT formats with nested claims.
resolution_steps: Hotfix deployed to break circular validation chain. Rolling restart of AuthService pods.

root_cause: Kong rate limit plugin configured with per-second limit instead of per-minute after configuration change. Legitimate traffic blocked.
resolution_steps: Updated Kong rate limit configuration to per-minute. Cleared rate limit counters. Verified traffic flowing normally.

root_cause: EKS worker node ran out of disk space causing kubelet to evict pods. PaymentAPI pods evicted and restarted causing brief service interruption.
resolution_steps: Drained and replaced affected node. Added disk pressure monitoring alerts. Increased node disk size.

root_cause: MongoDB Atlas connection pool timeout during maintenance window caused unhandled promise rejections in CartService. No retry logic in place.
resolution_steps: Increased MongoDB connection timeout. Added retry logic with exponential backoff to CartService MongoDB client.

root_cause: Missing index on product_category field caused full collection scans under high read load. ProductAPI response time exceeded 10 seconds.
resolution_steps: Added compound index on product_category and price fields. Query time dropped from 4 seconds to 12 milliseconds.

root_cause: Cloudflare API rate limit hit during bulk cache purge after product image update. Stale images served to users.
resolution_steps: Implemented batched cache purge with rate limiting. Stale images cleared within 30 minutes.

root_cause: Event listener not removed on session close causing memory leak. Node.js heap grew to 85 percent over 48 hours.
resolution_steps: Hotfix to remove event listeners on session close. Rolling restart of CartService pods.

root_cause: Flash sale traffic 5x normal volume exhausted MongoDB Atlas connection pool. CartService requests queued and timed out.
resolution_steps: Increased Atlas connection pool size. Added circuit breaker to CartService for MongoDB calls.

root_cause: Redis cluster rolling restart caused brief connection refused errors in ProductAPI. No retry logic in place for cache reads.
resolution_steps: Added Redis connection retry with backoff to ProductAPI. Implemented health check before cache reads.
